nodes.name: Countries of the World
nodes.description: Country sets by region and category

config.prompt: Name the 20 largest countries in the world by total area, in order from largest to smallest

items.explanation:
#1 largest country — ~17.1 million km², spanning 11 time zones
Nearly twice the size of #2 Canada; covers about 11% of Earth's total land area
Transcontinental across Europe and Asia; capital Moscow
The only country that borders both Norway and North Korea

#2 largest country — ~10.0 million km²
Has the world's longest coastline (~202,000 km) despite being only the second-largest country by area
Capital Ottawa; contains about 20% of the world's fresh surface water
Just barely larger than #3 United States; the gap is less than 3%

#3 largest country by total area — ~9.8 million km² (including significant inland water bodies like the Great Lakes)
Slightly larger than #4 China by total area, though China is larger by land area alone
Capital Washington, D.C.; spans 6 time zones across the contiguous states
The Great Lakes account for a substantial portion of the U.S. advantage over China in total area

#4 largest country by total area — ~9.6 million km²
Larger than the USA by land area alone, but the USA's extensive inland waters push it into third place on total area
Capital Beijing; world's most populous country until 2023 when India surpassed it
Borders 14 countries — more than any other country in the world

#5 largest country — ~8.5 million km²
Largest country in South America and in the Southern Hemisphere
Capital Brasília (purpose-built capital, inaugurated 1960); the Amazon basin covers about 40% of the country
Contains about 60% of the Amazon rainforest, the world's largest tropical rainforest

#6 largest country — ~7.7 million km²
The only country that is also an entire continent
Capital Canberra; population (~26 million) is heavily concentrated in coastal cities despite the vast interior
The interior 'Outback' is largely uninhabitable desert; Australia has the world's lowest population density among large countries

#7 largest country — ~3.3 million km²
Surpassed China as the world's most populous country in 2023 (~1.4 billion people)
Capital New Delhi; a major size drop from #6 Australia (~7.7M km²) to India (~3.3M km²)
The Indian subcontinent is separated from the rest of Asia by the Himalayas

#8 largest country — ~2.8 million km²
Second-largest country in South America and in the Southern Hemisphere
Capital Buenos Aires; stretches from the tropics in the north to near-Antarctica in the south (Tierra del Fuego)
Includes a claim to part of Antarctica and the Falkland Islands (disputed with the UK)

#9 largest country — ~2.7 million km²
World's largest landlocked country; larger than all of Western Europe combined
Capital Astana (renamed multiple times — was Nur-Sultan 2019–2022, Astana before and after)
Home to the Baikonur Cosmodrome, the world's first and largest operational space launch facility
Former Soviet republic; gained independence in 1991

#10 largest country — ~2.4 million km²
Largest country in Africa by area (since Sudan's partition in 2011)
Capital Algiers; about 90% of the country is covered by the Sahara Desert
French colony from 1830 until 1962; gained independence after a brutal war (1954–1962)

#11 largest country — ~2.3 million km²
Larger than Saudi Arabia, which surprises many; the DRC's size is often underestimated on standard maps
Capital Kinshasa; contains the Congo Rainforest, the world's second-largest tropical rainforest
The Congo River is the world's deepest river and second-largest by discharge (after the Amazon)
Has endured decades of conflict; one of the world's most resource-rich yet poorest countries

#12 largest country — ~2.15 million km²
Largest country in the Middle East; about 95% of its land is desert or semi-desert
Capital Riyadh; home to Islam's two holiest cities: Mecca and Medina
World's second-largest proven oil reserves; ARAMCO is the world's most profitable company
Greenland is larger (~2.2M km²) but is excluded here as it's an autonomous territory of Denmark, not a sovereign state

#13 largest country — ~2.0 million km²
Capital Mexico City, one of the world's largest metropolitan areas (~22 million)
Borders the United States to the north and Guatemala and Belize to the south
Home to more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country in the Americas
World's largest Spanish-speaking country by population

#14 largest country — ~1.9 million km²
World's largest archipelago nation — over 17,000 islands spanning 5,000 km
Capital Jakarta (government moving to new capital Nusantara on Borneo); world's fourth most populous country
Straddles the equator across Southeast Asia; home to the world's third-largest tropical rainforest
Has more active volcanoes than any other country

#15 largest country — ~1.86 million km²
Was Africa's largest country until South Sudan gained independence in 2011, which dropped Sudan to third-largest in Africa
Capital Khartoum, at the confluence of the Blue and White Nile rivers
Civil war broke out in 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the paramilitary RSF

#16 largest country — ~1.76 million km²
About 90% of the country is covered by the Sahara Desert; population heavily concentrated along the Mediterranean coast
Capital Tripoli; Italian colony from 1911 until WWII; gained independence in 1951
Dictator Muammar Gaddafi ruled from 1969 until he was killed in the 2011 Arab Spring uprising
Site of major WWII North Africa campaigns (Tobruk, El Alamein)

#17 largest country — ~1.65 million km²
Formerly known as Persia; renamed Iran in 1935
Capital Tehran; home to one of the world's oldest continuous civilizations
Borders the Caspian Sea, Persian Gulf, and Gulf of Oman but is not landlocked
Islamic Republic since the 1979 revolution that overthrew Shah Mohammad Reza Pahlavi

#18 largest country — ~1.56 million km²
World's most sparsely populated sovereign country at ~2 people per km²
Capital Ulaanbaatar, the world's coldest national capital
Home of the Mongol Empire (13th–14th century), the largest contiguous land empire in history
The Gobi Desert covers much of the south; the rest is steppe grassland ('the sea of grass')

#19 largest country — ~1.29 million km²
Capital Lima; heart of the ancient Inca Empire, the largest empire in pre-Columbian America
Machu Picchu (built c. 1450) is Peru's most visited site and a UNESCO World Heritage Site
Contains the Amazon headwaters; Lake Titicaca (shared with Bolivia) is the world's highest navigable lake
Just barely larger than #20 Chad

#20 largest country — ~1.28 million km²
Slightly smaller than #19 Peru; the two are nearly identical in area
Capital N'Djamena; located in north-central Africa, bordering 6 countries
Lake Chad, once one of Africa's largest lakes, has shrunk ~90% since the 1960s due to climate change and water diversion
One of the poorest and least developed countries in the world; mostly desert in the north, savanna in the south
